metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a body-level deliverable (e.g. in What or a new Documentation section) that explicitly names ADR-0042, describes the security-invariants section to be authored, and lists its required content (NoBlock grep + access diff), so AC6 has a covering work description.
findings.finding: AC6 requires writing a security-invariants artifact into ADR-0042, but ADR-0042 is not mentioned anywhere in the Why, What, or Scope sections — making AC6 an orphan criterion with no body-level description of the deliverable.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC6 / Plan body (Why, What, Scope sections)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the What section: 'Author an ADR-0042 Security Invariants section documenting (a) a `grep` of `infra/gerrit/project.config` confirming `function = NoBlock` and no submit-requirement reference, and (b) a `GET /projects/rebar/access` diff vs the pre-cutover snapshot confirming only `Autosubmit Voters` holds the `label-Autosubmit` vote permission.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether ADR-0042 must be **created** by this story (add it to scope) or whether AC6 should reference an already-existing document — ADR-0042 does not currently exist in `docs/adr/`, so the AC is unachievable as written.
findings.finding: The plan's AC6 mandates recording security invariants in 'the ADR-0042 Security invariants section', but ADR-0042 does not exist anywhere in the repository. The ADR directory tops out at ADR-0041; a global repo search for '0042' in docs/adr returns zero hits. The plan asserts a pre-existing document as the target artifact for a committed deliverable — if ADR-0042 hasn't been created, there is nowhere to record the invariant, and the AC is unachievable as written.
findings.location: AC6 — 'the ADR-0042 Security invariants section'
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) add 'Create `docs/adr/0042-autosubmit-label-security-invariants.md`' to the What/Scope sections and update AC6 to say 'create ADR-0042 recording…', or (b) if the intent is to append to an existing governance doc, replace 'ADR-0042 Security invariants section' in AC6 with the actual target file (e.g., an existing security-invariants runbook or the ADR-0041 security posture section).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the precondition that `autolander-bot` must be a pre-existing Gerrit account before `set-members --add autolander-bot` can succeed, OR add a `gerrit create-account autolander-bot` step (mirroring `service-user.sh`'s pattern) to `setup-project.sh` in this story's scope, OR explicitly reference the upstream story that provisions the account and note the dependency.
findings.finding: AC3 requires `setup-project.sh` to add `autolander-bot` to `Autosubmit Voters` via `gerrit set-members ... --add autolander-bot`, but `gerrit set-members --add <username>` fails with an error if that Gerrit account does not already exist. The plan's scope is explicitly 'Config + ACL + cutover ONLY' with no sibling bot-provisioning script for `autolander-bot` referenced or in scope, leaving the set-members convergence block silently broken on any environment where the account has not been pre-created out-of-band.
findings.location: AC3: 'setup-project.sh provisions/converges Autosubmit Voters: a gerrit create-group + set-members convergence block'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a paragraph to the What section naming the dependency: 'The `autolander-bot` Gerrit account must exist before setup-project.sh runs (provisioned by story SX). If it does not yet exist, add a `gerrit create-account autolander-bot --group "Autosubmit Voters"` step to setup-project.sh mirroring the service-user.sh pattern, protected by an `|| true` fallback for idempotency.' Alternatively, make the set-members block tolerate a missing account with an explicit warning rather than silent `|| true`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit pre-cutover step to AC 5 or the plan body: 'Before applying `refs/meta/config`, capture `GET /projects/rebar/access > pre-cutover-access.json`; the AC 6 diff is `diff pre-cutover-access.json <(GET /projects/rebar/access)` after cutover.'
findings.finding: AC 6 requires a diff of `GET /projects/rebar/access` against a 'pre-cutover snapshot', but no step in the plan prescribes capturing that snapshot before the cutover is applied. This is a missing prerequisite-state ordering gap (taxonomy item #7): the snapshot must exist before the cutover runs, yet it is never instructed.
findings.location: AC 6 — Security-invariants artifact
findings.suggested_fix: Insert at the start of AC 5: 'Step 0: `curl -u autolander-bot https://gerrit.example.com/a/projects/rebar/access > pre-cutover-access.json` — this snapshot is the baseline for the AC 6 diff.'

findings.finding: AC6 requires producing a diff of `GET /projects/rebar/access` vs a pre-cutover snapshot, but no step in the plan describes capturing the pre-cutover snapshot before changes are applied. Without the snapshot the diff cannot be produced, making the security-invariants artifact unverifiable.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC6 (Security-invariants artifact)
findings.suggested_fix: Insert a new step before the cutover: `curl -u admin:token https://gerrit.example/a/projects/rebar/access > infra/gerrit/snapshots/access-before-autosubmit.json` and reference this file in AC6 as the baseline for the diff.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly state in the ACL spec whether `group Administrators` should also receive `label-Autosubmit = -1..+1` (as a break-glass override matching the LLM-Review/Verified pattern), or whether it is deliberately excluded (strict bot-only); update the security-invariants AC to prove the intended posture holds against All-Projects inheritance.
findings.finding: The plan's ACL line (`label-Autosubmit = -1..+1 group Autosubmit Voters`) omits an explicit stance on whether `group Administrators` should also receive vote permission. Every existing vote label in project.config (LLM-Review, Verified) carries paired ACL lines: the functional group AND Administrators. The plan is silent on this, leaving an ambiguity: if Administrators inherit a vote grant from All-Projects, the 'bot-only ACL' claim in the security-invariants artifact (AC6) may be overstated; if they do not, admins lose a legitimate break-glass vote path. The plan needs to explicitly decide and state this either way.
findings.location: What section / ACL line: `[access "refs/heads/*"] label-Autosubmit = -1..+1 group Autosubmit Voters`; also AC line 2 and AC line 6 (security invariants artifact)
findings.suggested_fix: If strict bot-only is the intent, add `exclusiveGroupPermissions = label-Autosubmit` (or verify All-Projects has no Administrators vote grant for this label) and document it. If break-glass admin override is acceptable, add a second ACL line `label-Autosubmit = -1..+1 group Administrators` — mirroring the LLM-Review/Verified pattern — and reflect that in the security-invariants artifact.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the Administrators conflict: either add the explicit second ACL line (`label-Autosubmit = -1..+1 group Administrators`) to the body's canonical example, or remove `+ Administrators` from AC 2 if Administrators inherit the permission via Gerrit's group hierarchy.
findings.finding: The plan body's canonical ACL line (`label-Autosubmit = -1..+1 group Autosubmit Voters`) includes only `Autosubmit Voters`, but AC 2 adds `+ Administrators` to the restricted set. An executor must decide whether to add a second ACL line for the Administrators group — the plan gives no guidance on the line's exact form, whether it should be `-1..+1` or a narrower range, or whether it belongs in the same `[access]` stanza. This is a conflicting signal that blocks implementation.
findings.location: AC 2 vs. 'What' section ACL line
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the 'What' section a second ACL line: `label-Autosubmit = -1..+1 group Administrators` in the same `[access "refs/heads/*"]` stanza, and note that Administrators can retract/override the bot's vote for break-glass purposes. If Gerrit's built-in group inheritance already covers this, state 'Administrators inherit via global capability — no explicit line required' to close the ambiguity.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the auth mechanism and exact URL form (including the `/a/` prefix for authenticated Gerrit REST) for the `GET /projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit` and `GET /projects/rebar/access` verification calls in AC5 and AC6.
findings.finding: AC5 and AC6 both invoke Gerrit REST endpoints (`GET /projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit` and `GET /projects/rebar/access`) for post-cutover verification, but neither the plan body nor the ACs specify the auth mechanism (credentials/token type) for these REST calls. The codebase's only Gerrit REST usage pattern (smoke-check.sh) uses Basic Auth with the review-bot HTTP token fetched from SSM — a credential that may not have the access scope needed for admin-level `/projects/*/access` reads.
findings.location: AC5: 'verified via `GET /projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit`'; AC6: '`GET /projects/rebar/access` diff vs the pre-cutover snapshot'
findings.suggested_fix: Update AC5 to read: 'verified via `curl -u admin:<token> https://<host>/a/projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit`' (authenticated REST path) and AC6 similarly. Confirm that admin credentials (not just the review-bot token) are used for the access-diff step.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify AC2 to state explicitly whether Administrators require a separate `label-Autosubmit` ACL line or whether their global Gerrit capabilities provide sufficient coverage, and update the ACL line example accordingly.
findings.finding: AC2 states the vote ACL is restricted to 'group Autosubmit Voters (containing autolander-bot) + Administrators' but the ACL line shown only covers `Autosubmit Voters` — Administrators coverage is implied but not specified, leaving the implementer uncertain whether to add an explicit Administrators ACL line.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2
findings.suggested_fix: Replace '+ Administrators via [access "refs/heads/*"] label-Autosubmit = -1..+1 group Autosubmit Voters' with either: (a) 'No explicit Administrators line needed — Gerrit global Admin capability covers all labels' or (b) add a second ACL line: `label-Autosubmit = -1..+1 group Administrators`.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: AC #5 names `GET /projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit` as the post-cutover verification step, but this REST endpoint has never been exercised in the codebase (zero hits in the repo) and its auth form (/a/ Basic vs unauthenticated) and path sensitivity (the smoke-check.sh found a trailing-slash difference matters on this Gerrit instance) are unspecified — making it Partially-verified for an environment-specific probe.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria #5 — `GET /projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit`
findings.suggested_fix: Use `curl -sS -u "${GERRIT_SSH_USER}:${TOKEN}" "https://${GERRIT_HOST}/a/projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit"` (mirroring the authenticated pattern in smoke-check.sh line 42); add a trailing-slash variant as a fallback probe and document which form is canonical for this Gerrit version.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC that exercises the live enforcement boundary: (a) cast `Autosubmit=+1` as `autolander-bot` on a scratch change and assert HTTP 200, and (b) attempt `Autosubmit=+1` as a non-member account and assert HTTP 403, both against the production Gerrit instance after cutover.
findings.finding: No acceptance criterion exercises the live ACL enforcement boundary — specifically, (a) a vote cast by `autolander-bot` is accepted, and (b) a vote cast by an ordinary user is rejected — against the live `refs/meta/config` after cutover. `GET /projects/rebar/access` proves the ACL is configured, but not that it is enforced. This is a cutover with no end-to-end production-path exercise per E6(f).
findings.location: AC 2, AC 5
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC 5: 'Verified by: (a) `ssh -p 29418 autolander-bot@gerrit gerrit review --label "Autosubmit=+1" <scratch-change>,1` returns exit 0; (b) the same command as a non-member account returns exit 1 / HTTP 403 from the REST API.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Anchor the AC 6 access-diff assertion with a concrete `jq -e` path, e.g., `jq -e '.local["refs/heads/*"].permissions["label-Autosubmit"].rules | keys == ["<Autosubmit-Voters-UUID>"]'` against the post-cutover `GET /projects/rebar/access` response.
findings.finding: AC 6's `GET /projects/rebar/access` diff is described in prose ('proving only Autosubmit Voters can vote the label') but no structured assertion anchor (e.g., `jq -e`) is specified. This risks a false-positive pass if the diff output is large and the relevant field is buried or named differently than expected (taxonomy item #6: prose-vs-structured assertion target).
findings.location: AC 6 — Security-invariants artifact

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out procedure to the plan: at minimum, a pre-cutover snapshot of refs/meta/config, a `git revert` or `git push ... <prior-sha>` command to restore it, and a post-rollback verification step.
findings.finding: The plan describes a live cutover to refs/meta/config (AC5: 'live cutover applied') but provides no rollback or back-out procedure if the cutover introduces an unintended ACL regression or config-check failure in production.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC5 / What section ('operator cutover')
findings.suggested_fix: Add a Rollback section: 'Before cutover, capture `git fetch origin refs/meta/config && git show FETCH_HEAD > pre-cutover-snapshot.txt`. If cutover fails, restore with `git push origin <pre-cutover-sha>:refs/meta/config` and re-run `GET /projects/rebar/access` to confirm revert.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Create (or extend an existing design doc / ADR) to record the `Autosubmit` label-as-agent-opt-in-lease pattern: the name-collision rationale (project-authored vs. Gerrit first-party plugin), the non-gating design choice and its FFO justification, and the canonical `autolander-bot` / `Autosubmit Voters` naming that downstream stories must reference — so the contract is discoverable independently of this story's prose.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel architectural pattern — the `Autosubmit` label as a machine-readable agent opt-in lease (including the name-collision rationale, non-gating design choice, and canonical account/group naming that downstream stories depend on) — but documents only the security-invariants slice in ADR-0042, leaving the label contract/pattern itself without a dedicated ADR or design-doc entry.
findings.location: ## Why / ## What / AC item 6 (ADR-0042 Security invariants section)
findings.suggested_fix: Add an ADR (e.g. ADR-0043) or a `docs/design/autosubmit-label.md` that captures: (1) why the label is project-authored and uppercase, (2) the non-gating / NoBlock choice and its FFO dependency, (3) the canonical bot identity and group name, and (4) a pointer to ADR-0042 for security invariants. Reference it from the epic and each downstream story.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback step to the cutover procedure: record the pre-cutover SHA of refs/meta/config and specify `git push origin <prev-sha>:refs/meta/config` as the recovery action if post-cutover GET verification fails.
findings.finding: The cutover to refs/meta/config has no explicit rollback or recovery procedure if the push fails mid-way or if post-cutover verification (GET /projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit) reveals a bad state. Gerrit ref pushes are atomic at the ref level, but the plan does not specify what the operator should do if config-check passes yet the live verification fails, leaving the system in an uncertain state.
findings.location: AC line 5: 'config-check passes; live cutover applied to refs/meta/config and verified via GET /projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit'
findings.suggested_fix: In the cutover runbook section, add: (1) capture pre-cutover SHA via `git rev-parse refs/meta/config` before pushing; (2) if `GET /projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit` returns non-200 or missing label after push, immediately revert with `git push origin <captured-sha>:refs/meta/config --force` and re-run config-check.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify an observability hook for the new ACL: either a periodic smoke-test (e.g., a canary that checks `GET /a/changes/.../reviewers` or attempts a dry-run vote as autolander-bot) with an alert on failure, or document that the downstream bot story must emit a labeled metric/log on 403 responses to label-Autosubmit.
findings.finding: There is no observability specification for the new ACL failure mode: if the Autosubmit Voters group is misconfigured or the autolander-bot account is not a member, the bot will silently fail to vote the label. The plan defines no metric, log line, or alert to detect this condition.
findings.location: What section: 'Vote ACL restricted to the concrete bot identity: service account autolander-bot, in group Autosubmit Voters'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a RETIRE section (even a one-liner) specifying how to remove the label: delete the `[label "Autosubmit"]` stanza and the `label-Autosubmit` access line from project.config, push to refs/meta/config, and empty/delete the Autosubmit Voters group via `gerrit set-members --remove`.
findings.finding: The label and Autosubmit Voters group have no defined retirement or rollback path. If the feature is disabled, rolled back, or superseded, there is no specified procedure to remove the label from project.config, revoke the ACL entry, or delete/empty the group — leaving orphaned config that could confuse future operators or be re-exploited.
findings.location: Scope section: 'Config + ACL + cutover ONLY'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the `copyCondition` capability assertion: state the minimum supported Gerrit version (≥ 3.4) in the plan, OR add a cutover verification step that confirms the vote is absent after a new patchset is uploaded (e.g., `GET /changes/{id}/revisions/2/votes` shows no `Autosubmit` entry), so the non-sticky behavior is empirically verified rather than assumed.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that an empty `copyCondition` causes the label to drop on every new patchset, but this is a Gerrit-version-dependent capability claim. In Gerrit < 3.4, vote copying was controlled by legacy boolean flags (`copyAllScoresIfNoChange`, `copyAllScoresOnTrivialRebase`, etc.), not `copyCondition`; the `copyCondition` field only became the canonical mechanism in Gerrit 3.4+. If the target Gerrit instance is < 3.4, an absent/empty `copyCondition` stanza would not be sufficient to guarantee non-sticky behavior, and the plan provides no Gerrit version pin or verification step to confirm the assertion holds.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1: 'empty `copyCondition` (drops on every new patchset)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a line to AC5 (config-check / cutover verification): 'Upload a test patchset to a scratch change carrying `Autosubmit=+1`; verify via `GET /changes/{id}/revisions/{n+1}/votes` that the `Autosubmit` vote is absent on the new revision, confirming non-sticky behavior on the live Gerrit version.' Also add a note in the What section: 'Requires Gerrit ≥ 3.4 for `copyCondition` semantics; verify instance version before cutover.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm the prior-art dismissal of Gerrit's `autosubmit` submit-plugin: cite one concrete configuration option or plugin documentation reference showing the plugin cannot be used in a signal-only (non-submitting) mode, or explicitly acknowledge that the plugin architecture was reviewed and ruled out because it always performs the submit action.
findings.finding: The plan dismisses Gerrit's first-party `autosubmit` submit-plugin on the grounds that it 'auto-submits on gate satisfaction, which is not what we want,' but does not verify whether the plugin supports a mode where it only acts as a label signal (read by a separate bot) rather than performing the submit itself. Some Gerrit plugin configurations allow the plugin label to be consumed externally. If the plugin could be configured in a read-only / signal-only capacity, authoring a project-level label would be unnecessary duplication — the prior-art dismissal is asserted without citing plugin documentation or configuration options.
findings.location: Why section — 'Name collision' callout box

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a drift-detection step — e.g., a CI check that diffs the checked-in `infra/gerrit/project.config` against a `git fetch` of the live `refs/meta/config` and fails the pipeline on divergence, or document an explicit reconciliation procedure in the runbook.
findings.finding: The plan describes a one-time 'operator cutover' of infra/gerrit/project.config into live refs/meta/config but specifies no mechanism to detect or reconcile drift if a Gerrit admin subsequently edits refs/meta/config out-of-band via the Gerrit UI or a direct git push.
findings.location: AC line 5 / 'live cutover applied to refs/meta/config'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a CI job that fetches `refs/meta/config` from the live Gerrit instance, extracts the `[label "Autosubmit"]` and relevant `[access]` stanzas, and diffs them against `infra/gerrit/project.config`; fail on divergence. Alternatively, gate all `refs/meta/config` changes through the same PR+config-check pipeline.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify a pre-production validation step — either a staging/test Gerrit project where the full label+ACL config is applied and exercised before the production cutover, or a documented pre-apply checklist that includes a `GET /projects/rebar/access` diff against the pre-cutover snapshot to confirm only the intended change is present.
findings.finding: The plan applies the new ACL and label directly to the production refs/meta/config with only a `config-check` as a safeguard; no staging Gerrit instance or pre-production dry-run validation step is specified.
findings.location: AC line 5 / 'Scope: Config + ACL + cutover ONLY'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document a rollback procedure: capture the pre-cutover `refs/meta/config` SHA, specify the exact `git push` command to revert it, and include this in the operator runbook or the AC.
findings.finding: The plan provides no rollback procedure for the refs/meta/config cutover; if the new label or ACL produces unintended side effects post-deploy, there is no specified revert path.
findings.location: AC line 5 / 'live cutover applied to refs/meta/config'
findings.suggested_fix: Before the cutover, record `git rev-parse refs/meta/config` as ROLLBACK_SHA and add to the AC: 'On failure, execute `git push origin ROLLBACK_SHA:refs/meta/config` to revert.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a rollback procedure to the cutover section: specify the pre-cutover `refs/meta/config` SHA capture step, the exact revert command (`git push origin <pre-cutover-sha>:refs/meta/config`), and an explicit assertion of the expected post-rollback schema state (label block absent, ACL entry absent, `GET /projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit` returns 404).
findings.finding: The plan specifies a live cutover to `refs/meta/config` but never defines a rollback procedure or asserts the post-rollback schema state. The pre-cutover snapshot is referenced only as a diff baseline for security verification, not as a recoverable artifact with a documented restore step. If the cutover is partially applied or yields an unexpected Gerrit state, there is no stated back-out path and no assertion of what `refs/meta/config` looks like after rollback.
findings.location: AC: `config-check` passes; live cutover applied to `refs/meta/config` and verified via `GET /projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit`; also Security-invariants AC referencing pre-cutover snapshot
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Rollback' subsection under the cutover AC: (1) before pushing, capture `PRE=$(git ls-remote <gerrit-url> refs/meta/config | awk '{print $1}')`; (2) if post-cutover verification fails, run `git push origin ${PRE}:refs/meta/config --force`; (3) assert post-rollback state: label absent from `GET /projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit` (404) and ACL diff returns empty vs pre-cutover snapshot.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Tighten the `label-Autosubmit` vote ACL from `refs/heads/*` to `refs/heads/main` (or the declared landing target) to satisfy least-privilege (T5c); if multi-branch landing is genuinely intended, document the rationale explicitly in the plan.
findings.finding: The vote ACL is scoped to `refs/heads/*` (all branches) but the plan's entire rationale is FFO `main`-only landing. Granting `Autosubmit Voters` the ability to cast `label-Autosubmit` on arbitrary feature/release/personal branches over-grants rights beyond the declared use-case; a future consumer or a compromised bot vote on a non-`main` ref could trigger unintended auto-land behaviour.
findings.location: What section / AC-2: `[access "refs/heads/*"] label-Autosubmit = -1..+1 group Autosubmit Voters`
findings.suggested_fix: Change the ACL stanza to `[access "refs/heads/main"] label-Autosubmit = -1..+1 group Autosubmit Voters` in both `project.config` and the AC-2 specification. If other branches must be supported, enumerate them explicitly rather than using the wildcard.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Declare the `autolander-bot` credential lifecycle in this plan (or explicitly defer it with a named follow-on story): specify the issuance mechanism (e.g., Gerrit HTTP password vs OAuth token), the secrets-manager that stores it (e.g., Vault, GCP Secret Manager), and the rotation policy — satisfying T5c secret-lifecycle for the boundary-crossing bot identity.
findings.finding: The plan provisions the `autolander-bot` service account into the security-sensitive `Autosubmit Voters` group (via `setup-project.sh`) but never declares how that account's Gerrit credentials are managed: no mention of secrets-manager storage, rotation policy, or issuance mechanism. Because the bot authenticates to Gerrit over the network to cast a vote that triggers code landing, this is a live boundary-crossing secret-lifecycle gap within the plan's stated scope.
findings.location: What section / AC-3: `setup-project.sh` provisions `Autosubmit Voters` and `set-members` with `autolander-bot`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a fallback/rollback sub-step to AC-5 specifying the revert procedure (e.g., `git push origin HEAD:refs/meta/config` with the pre-cutover snapshot) and the decision rule for when to trigger it (config-check failure OR verification GET returns unexpected result).
findings.finding: The cutover step in AC-5 ('live cutover applied to refs/meta/config and verified via GET /projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit') specifies no rollback or fallback procedure if config-check fails or if the push to refs/meta/config is rejected/partially applied. For an operator cutover that modifies live ACLs, the absence of a defined recovery path means an executor has no protocol to follow on failure.
findings.location: AC-5: 'config-check passes; live cutover applied to refs/meta/config and verified via GET /projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit'
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC-5 to: 'config-check passes; live cutover applied to refs/meta/config; verified via GET /projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit. **Rollback:** if config-check fails or the GET verification does not return function=NoBlock with the expected ACL, revert by pushing the pre-cutover refs/meta/config snapshot (`git push origin <pre-cutover-sha>:refs/meta/config --force-with-lease`) and re-open the story.'

findings.finding: The plan specifies adding `Autosubmit Voters` to `setup-project.sh`'s `want=` dict (AC #3), but the existing provisioning pattern requires a separate `gerrit create-group` block for any group referenced in `project.config` — without it, the `want=` lookup returns nothing and Gerrit rejects the `refs/meta/config` push with an 'unknown group' error. The plan names only the `want=` dict as the edit target and omits the `create-group` stanza as a required edit.
findings.location: AC #3 / infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh lines 60–107
findings.suggested_fix: Add a block immediately before the `want=` dict entry (around line 108) following the same pattern as lines 91–107: `AS_GROUP="Autosubmit Voters"; if ! $GERRIT_SSH gerrit ls-groups | grep -qxF -- "$AS_GROUP"; then $GERRIT_SSH gerrit create-group "$AS_GROUP" --description "Autosubmit label voters: autolander-bot only" --owner Administrators; fi; $GERRIT_SSH gerrit set-members "$AS_GROUP" --add autolander-bot >/dev/null 2>&1 || true`. Then include `["Autosubmit Voters"]=1` in the `want=` dict.

findings.finding: The plan specifies adding `Autosubmit Voters` to the `want=` UUID dict in setup-project.sh (so the group UUID is resolved for the refs/meta/config push), but omits specifying that a corresponding group-creation-and-membership-convergence block must also be added. Without it, if `Autosubmit Voters` doesn't yet exist on the server, `gerrit ls-groups --verbose` returns no line for it, the UUID is empty, and Gerrit rejects the refs/meta/config push with an 'unknown group' error — the same failure mode the existing script comments explicitly guard against for feature-branch-drivers and Contributors.
findings.location: What section — 'Register the group's UUID in `setup-project.sh`'s `want=` group dict'
findings.suggested_fix:
Add a block to setup-project.sh immediately before the `want=` declaration:
```bash
AV_GROUP="Autosubmit Voters"
if $GERRIT_SSH gerrit ls-groups | grep -qxF -- "$AV_GROUP"; then
  echo "setup-project: group '$AV_GROUP' already exists" >&2
else
  echo "setup-project: creating group '$AV_GROUP'" >&2
  $GERRIT_SSH gerrit create-group "$AV_GROUP" \
    --description "Accounts authorised to cast Autosubmit votes (autolander-bot only)"
fi
$GERRIT_SSH gerrit set-members "$AV_GROUP" --add autolander-bot >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
```
Then add `["Autosubmit Voters"]=1` to the `want=` dict.

findings.finding: The plan specifies adding 'Autosubmit Voters' to setup-project.sh's `want=` group-UUID dict (AC #3), but tool-verified inspection of setup-project.sh shows that the `want=` dict ONLY resolves UUIDs of groups that ALREADY EXIST on the server — it does not create them. The existing pattern (feature-branch-drivers, Contributors) requires an explicit `gerrit create-group` block BEFORE the `want=` step, or Gerrit will reject the refs/meta/config push with an 'unknown group' error. The plan omits this prerequisite creation protocol entirely.
findings.location: AC #3 / infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh line 123
findings.suggested_fix: Following the Contributors pattern (lines 91-107 of setup-project.sh), add: (1) an AUTOSUBMIT_VOTERS_GROUP var, (2) a create-group block with --description and --owner Administrators, (3) a set-members call to add autolander-bot, then (4) the existing want= entry. Reference this creation block in AC #3.

findings.finding: The plan specifies adding `Autosubmit Voters` to `setup-project.sh`'s `want=` group-UUID dict (AC #3) but is silent on the preceding `gerrit create-group 'Autosubmit Voters'` + `gerrit set-members` convergence block that must run before UUID resolution. The existing script (lines 47–107) shows the invariant: every group in `project.config` must have a `create-group`-if-absent + `set-members` block before the `want=` dict attempts to resolve its UUID via `ls-groups`. Without this block, `ls-groups` will not list `Autosubmit Voters`, no UUID row is written to the `groups` file, and Gerrit rejects the `refs/meta/config` push with an 'unknown group' error — breaking the cutover entirely.
findings.location: What section ('Register the group's UUID in `setup-project.sh`'s `want=` group dict') and AC #3
findings.suggested_fix: Add a paragraph to the What section (after 'Register the group's UUID…'): 'In `setup-project.sh`, add an `Autosubmit Voters` provisioning block — identical in structure to the `feature-branch-drivers` block (lines 60–74) — that (a) creates the group if absent (`gerrit create-group "Autosubmit Voters" --description "…" --group Administrators`), (b) idempotently adds `autolander-bot` via `gerrit set-members "Autosubmit Voters" --add autolander-bot`, and (c) adds `"Autosubmit Voters"` to the `want=` dict so its UUID is resolved and written to the `groups` file before the `refs/meta/config` push.'

findings.finding: The plan's `setup-project.sh` edit is described only as adding `Autosubmit Voters` to the `want=` UUID dict (line 123), but the script also needs a new `create-group` + `set-members` stanza (parallel to the `feature-branch-drivers` and `Contributors` blocks at lines 60–107) to provision the group before the refs/meta/config push. Without the group-creation stanza, Gerrit will reject the push with 'unknown group', as the script's own comment at lines 51–52 explains.
findings.location: What section: 'Register the group's UUID in `setup-project.sh`'s `want=` group dict' / AC3
findings.suggested_fix: Add a stanza to setup-project.sh section 1b: create `Autosubmit Voters` if absent (`gerrit create-group "Autosubmit Voters" --owner Administrators`), then add `autolander-bot` via `gerrit set-members "Autosubmit Voters" --add autolander-bot` (idempotent). Add `["Autosubmit Voters"]=1` to the `want=` dict.

findings.finding: AC #3 specifies only adding `Autosubmit Voters` to `setup-project.sh`'s `want=` group-UUID dict, but the existing provisioning pattern (observed in setup-project.sh lines 60–107) requires three distinct steps for any group referenced in project.config: (1) `gerrit create-group` if absent, (2) `gerrit set-members` to converge membership, and (3) UUID resolution via `ls-groups --verbose` into the `want=` dict. Without steps 1–2, if `Autosubmit Voters` does not pre-exist on the server, Gerrit will reject the refs/meta/config push with an 'unknown group' error — the same failure mode the existing code's comment explicitly guards against (setup-project.sh line 53–54).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria #3 — `Autosubmit Voters` group UUID in `setup-project.sh`'s `want=` dict
findings.suggested_fix: Add a provisioning block to setup-project.sh immediately before the `want=` declaration, modelled exactly on lines 60–107: check `gerrit ls-groups | grep -qxF 'Autosubmit Voters'`, create if absent with `gerrit create-group 'Autosubmit Voters' --description '...' --owner Administrators`, then converge membership with `gerrit set-members 'Autosubmit Voters' --add autolander-bot`, and finally add `["Autosubmit Voters"]=1` to `want=`.

findings.finding: The plan specifies adding 'Autosubmit Voters' to setup-project.sh's `want=` group-UUID dict, but does not specify the corresponding create-if-absent + membership-converge block for the group itself. Without a `gerrit create-group 'Autosubmit Voters'` + `gerrit set-members` block (analogous to the existing feature-branch-drivers and Contributors blocks), the `want=` entry will fail to resolve a UUID — causing Gerrit to reject the refs/meta/config push with an 'unknown group' error — on any run where the group hasn't been pre-created out-of-band.
findings.location: What section (setup-project.sh `want=` dict) / AC#3
findings.suggested_fix:
Insert a group-provisioning block in setup-project.sh (after the Contributors block, before step 2) following the existing pattern:
```bash
AV_GROUP="Autosubmit Voters"
if $GERRIT_SSH gerrit ls-groups | grep -qxF -- "$AV_GROUP"; then
  echo "setup-project: group '$AV_GROUP' already exists" >&2
else
  echo "setup-project: creating group '$AV_GROUP'" >&2
  $GERRIT_SSH gerrit create-group "$AV_GROUP" \
    --description "Accounts permitted to cast Autosubmit label votes (autolander-bot only)" \
    --owner Administrators
fi
$GERRIT_SSH gerrit set-members "$AV_GROUP" --include Administrators >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
for _m in ${AUTOSUBMIT_VOTER_MEMBERS:-autolander-bot}; do
  $GERRIT_SSH gerrit set-members "$AV_GROUP" --add "$_m" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
done
```
Then add `["Autosubmit Voters"]=1` to the `want=` dict on line 123.

findings.finding: The AC #1 value vocabulary for the -1 slot is conditional ('if the low end is used'), but the label range is already fixed at -1..+1 in both the label definition and the ACL grant — meaning the -1 value IS always reachable by Autosubmit Voters. The enum string for this reachable value is therefore never definitively defined, leaving the executor to choose a display string for a voting option that the system will accept.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC #1: 'value = -1 Do not land (if the low end is used)'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'value = -1 Do not land (if the low end is used)' with 'value = -1 Do not land' — the range is fixed and the value is always present.

findings.finding: The AC wording for the -1 value line is conditional ('if the low end is used'), but the label range is already fixed at -1..+1 in both the ACL line and the label definition. This ambiguity could lead an implementer to omit the `value = -1 Do not land` line while the ACL still allows -1 votes, producing a Gerrit config with a vote value that has no display label.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1: 'value = -1 Do not land if the low end is used'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `value = -1 Do not land (if the low end is used)` with `value = -1 Do not land` as a required (non-conditional) AC1 line, matching the fixed -1..+1 range already specified in the label definition and ACL.

findings.finding: AC1 uses 'if the low end is used' to make the `value = -1 Do not land` line conditional, but the ACL range is explicitly `-1..+1`, making the -1 value non-optional. An executor could omit the -1 value line and still satisfy the literal AC wording while producing a misconfigured label.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1
findings.suggested_fix: Change the AC1 parenthetical to: 'and explicit value lines: `value = -1 Do not land`, `value = 0 No score`, `value = +1 Land this change`' (unconditional).

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
